key_insight: SIGMA improves multi-agent design by constructing agents from reusable skills, rather than only optimizing communication links between fixed agents.
engineering_relevance: This compositional approach is relevant for engineering workflows because it can better adapt agent teams to novel tasks and changing skill requirements.
method: Predict a skill-agent incidence matrix from the task and skill library.
Compose each agent embedding from selected skills, then decode a communication topology over these constructed agents.
Use skill-specific mailboxes so messages are routed to the capabilities actually assigned to each agent.
evidence_signal: Across six reasoning and coding benchmarks with three base LLMs, SIGMA achieved the best average performance and outperformed CARD by 2.06, 2.36, and 1.75 points, with only a 0.96-point average drop on unseen skill libraries.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and coverage of the skill library, so missing or poorly defined skills could limit transfer to new tasks.
paper_title: SIGMA: Skill-Incidence Graphs for Compositional Multi-Agent Design
